Bus Timings

Bus 77 in Sharjah: Route and Stops Explained

February 28, 2026
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus 77 is a vital public transportation service in Sharjah, connecting Al Saja’a Terminal to Sharjah — Al Muwaileh Terminal. This bus route primarily serves commuters and residents traveling between the industrial areas and the educational and residential zones in Sharjah. It offers a reliable and frequent service, ensuring that passengers can reach their destinations efficiently.

Route Name 77
Route Description Al Saja’a Terminal - Sharjah — Al Muwaileh Terminal
Total Distance Approximately 25 kilometers
Total Journey Time Approximately 30-60 minutes
Operational Hours 05:45 to 23:40 (Depending on Starting Point)
Fare AED 8 (Cash) / AED 6 (Sayer Card)
Official Website SRTA Gov

Download PDF Bus 77

Bus Timings

Bus 77 operates with a consistent schedule throughout the day, ensuring frequent service. Here are the departure times:

  • From Sharjah — Al Muwaileh Terminal: 06:00, 06:25, 06:50, 07:15, 07:40, 08:05, 08:30, 08:55, 09:20, 09:45, 10:10, 10:35, 11:00, 11:25, 11:50, 12:15, 12:40, 13:05, 13:30, 13:55, 14:20, 14:45, 15:10, 15:35, 16:00, 16:25, 16:50, 17:15, 17:40, 18:05, 18:30, 18:55, 19:20, 19:45, 20:10, 20:35, 21:00, 21:25, 21:50, 22:15, 22:40, 23:05

Bus Ticket Pric

The fare for Bus 77 is aligned with Sharjah’s standard public transportation rates. Passengers can use their Sayer card to pay for their trips, making it easy and convenient. The cost per trip is AED 8 if paid in cash, or AED 6 with a Sayer Card, offering a more economical option for frequent travelers.

Bus Booking

For real-time updates on bus schedules, routes, and any potential changes, passengers are encouraged to use the official Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA) app or website.

Bus Route

The Bus 77 route covers several key locations, starting from Al Saja’a Terminal and ending at Sharjah — Al Muwaileh Terminal. Below is a detailed list of the stops along the way:

  1. Al Saja’a Terminal

  2. Sajaa Ministry of Labor Office

  3. Sajaa, F

  4. Al Kher Mousque, F

  5. Cement Factory, F

  6. Sharjah Cement Factory

  7. Sharjah Cement Factory

  8. Sharjah Cement Factory / National Cable

  9. Al Atain

  10. Al Elm, 4

  11. Al Elm, 3

  12. Al Elm, 2

  13. Maliha Road

  14. Noaf, 2

  15. Noaf

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Noaf Airport Road Interchange

  2. Abu Dhabi Vocational Education Training Institute

  3. Maliha Road, Sharjah Institute of Technology

  4. Sharjah English School

  5. Maliha Road, Industrial 17 Exit

  6. Maliha Road, Apollo Medical

  7. Maliha Road, Delhi Private School

  8. Sharjah — Al Muwaileh Terminal

This route is strategically designed to connect various industrial and residential areas, making it convenient for both workers and students.

Explore the bus schedule and fare details for your journey from Sharjah to Salalah.

Bus 77 Station Overview

Al Saja’a Terminal

Al Saja’a Terminal is a significant bus station located in the industrial area of Sharjah. This terminal serves as a major transit point for workers and commuters traveling to and from the bustling industrial zones. Its strategic location makes it a crucial hub for those needing to access the industrial sector, offering connections to various bus routes that serve the broader region.

Sharjah — Al Muwaileh Terminal

Passengers traveling on these two routes have the convenience of transferring between them at this station.

This intersection allows for seamless travel across Sharjah and to neighboring emirates, enhancing overall accessibility and mobility for public transport users.

Tourist Destination Along the Way

The Bus 77 route in Sharjah primarily serves industrial areas, educational institutions, and residential zones. While the route does not directly pass through major tourist attractions, here are a few points of interest near the bus route:

  1. Sharjah National Park: Although not directly on the bus route, this park is a short drive from Maliha Road and offers green spaces, picnic areas, and playgrounds, making it a popular spot for families and visitors looking for a peaceful outdoor experience.

2.

  1. Sharjah Archaeology Museum: While not directly on the route, this museum offers a glimpse into Sharjah’s rich history and archaeological findings. It’s an interesting stop for those interested in the history of the UAE.

  2. University City of Sharjah: Located nearby, this area is home to several universities and colleges, including the American University of Sharjah and the University of Sharjah. While primarily educational, the architecture and campus atmosphere are worth exploring.

  3. Sharjah Cricket Stadium: Located a short distance from the terminal, this stadium is a key venue for cricket matches and events in the region. Visitors interested in sports can check the schedule for upcoming matches or events.

While Bus 77 serves practical commuting needs, these nearby attractions offer opportunities for leisure and exploration.

Travelling with Careem

Using the Careem app, you can conveniently travel to Sharjah — Al Muwaileh Terminal, the furthest point on the 77 bus route starting from the Al Saja’a Terminal. Although Al Saja’a Terminal cannot be directly pinpointed in the app, you can select a nearby location along the E88 route as your starting point. The fare for this journey typically ranges between AED 59-73, offering a flexible and affordable alternative for reaching your destination.

Bus 77 serves as a crucial link between Al Saja’a Terminal and Sharjah — Al Muwaileh Terminal, connecting industrial zones, educational institutions, and residential areas. Bus 77 ensures a convenient and reliable travel option for both daily commuters and occasional travelers.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)
